vBRIEFInfo.author: overdeck/0.0.0
vBRIEFInfo.description: Plan for PAN-1791: Tiered execution — difficulty-routed model tiers, standing warm tier agents, event-driven supervisor (folds in PAN-1196 difficulty/subject routing + PAN-1311 trivial fast-track)

plan.title: Tiered execution: difficulty-routed bead dispatch + event-driven supervisor review (standing tier agents with commit feeds)
plan.author: agent:claude-opus-4-8

narratives.Problem: Every bead in an issue runs on the same model even though each bead is labeled with a difficulty — the label is captured and ignored (PAN-1196). There is no way to keep a spectrum of model tiers warm and route each bead to the tier its difficulty deserves, no event-driven supervisor reviewing work at commit boundaries, and trivial mechanical items pay full swarm-slot overhead (PAN-1311). Expensive-model attention is a participant, not an interrupt handler.
narratives.Proposal: Build on the PAN-1762 foreman (prompt-driven wave-driver in roles/work.md + helper libs; chooseDispatchTier is its seam). (A) Generalize the claude-only, hardcoded difficulty->model routing into a configurable any-model/any-harness tier table with a resolution chain (per-bead override -> byKind -> byDifficulty -> role default), and wire it into dispatch so difficulty actually selects the working model. (B) Keep one long-lived registered slot per tier alive for the issue, warmed by a commit feed that broadcasts every commit's diff to all standing tiers (everyone hears everything). (C) Add a standing frontier supervisor subscribed to commit events that reviews each diff against the bead's acceptance criteria and posts an ack or a blocking finding on the existing inspect-status surface, halting dependent dispatch until a fix lands. (D) Fast-track trivial mechanical beads: batch them, skip the convoy, typecheck-gated auto-merge, with fail-open escalation. (E) Replay (respawn + re-deliver the commit feed from git log) doubles as crash recovery and threshold-triggered context compaction. (F) Measurement scaffolding: stamp feed deliveries and attribute cost per tier. Everything off by default (tiered_execution.enabled=false).
narratives.NonGoals:
- Plan-filtered / per-tier commit relevance filtering (everyone hears everything in v1; filtering is a deferred cost optimization).
- Running the live cost pilot or writing the tiered-vs-monolithic savings report (post-merge operational activity, not code).
- Remote (Fly) tier agents (PAN-1676); design must not preclude them but v1 runs tiers locally.
- Multiple models autonomously deciding to intervene (the router decides by difficulty).
- In-session model switching (tiers are separate standing sessions; fresh-session invariant stands).
- Replacing the convoy review pipeline at pan done (the supervisor complements it).
- Rebuilding any deleted server-side swarm runtime (PAN-1517 removed swarmRuntime; do not resurrect it).
- Adding a hardcoded model fallback anywhere (unset resolution must fail loud, not pick a literal).

autoDecisions.summary: Everyone-hears-everything (no per-tier commit filtering) in v1.
autoDecisions.rationale: Operator decision; live evidence shows listening is ~1-5% of implementer cost with prompt caching, so the filtering complexity can be deferred until measurement justifies it.

autoDecisions.summary: Standing tier agent = long-lived registered slot (spawnRun) kept warm by the feed, riding the as-built per-slot-branch + verify-then-merge model.
autoDecisions.rationale: The PAN-1762 foreman shipped parallel slots, superseding the PRD's shared-worktree/foreman-commits D9/D12; reuse what landed instead of a second model.

autoDecisions.summary: 4 beads carry requiresInspection=true (tier-table config, resolution chain, standing-tier lifecycle, fast-track auto-merge).
autoDecisions.rationale: Three are genuine interface foundations other beads consume; the auto-merge bead crosses a review/trust boundary. All others are leaves or test-verified, so kept false.

autoDecisions.summary: This issue is implemented BY the swarm; every bead carries the full dispatch contract and file ownership is designed so no two parallel beads share a file.
autoDecisions.rationale: Operator directed swarm execution; maximizes parallelism while the readiness lint + blocks edges keep hotspot files (roles/work.md, dispatch-tier.ts, fast-track.ts, tier-supervisor.ts, standing-tiers.ts, tier-feed.ts) serialized.

items.title: Tier table config section + validation (generalizes complexity_routing) + supervisor policy, off by default

metadata.parallelSafeReason: Wave 0. Sole owner of the new tier-table.ts and the tiered_execution config block; no concurrent bead writes these files.

narrative.Action: Create src/lib/agents/tier-table.ts defining a `tiered_execution` config shape: { enabled: boolean (default false), tiers: Record<tierName,{model,harness,difficulties: VBriefDifficulty[]}>, supervisor: {model,harness,subscribe:'all'|'flagged'|'sampled'}, replay_threshold: number (default 0.5) }. Add the section to the cloister config (src/lib/cloister/config.ts) and config-yaml defaults/merge so it loads and merges. Write a loader+validator: every difficulty maps to exactly one tier (total, non-overlapping); unknown model/harness rejected at load; the pi+Anthropic+subscription ToS gate (src/lib/harness-policy.ts) applies to tier and supervisor definitions. This is the schema the rest of the feature consumes.

items.title: Given a config where a difficulty maps to zero or to two tiers, loading rejects it with a named validation error identifying the offending difficulty
Given a tier with an unknown model or harness, loading rejects it, and a pi+Anthropic+subscription tier is rejected by the existing ToS gate
With no tiered_execution block present, the loader returns enabled=false and defaults to replay_threshold=0.5 without error
For a valid config, the validator returns a map from each of the five difficulties to its tier name plus the supervisor policy
Per-issue tiered_execution override — issue-level opt-in/out from vBRIEF metadata, overriding the global flag

metadata.parallelSafeReason: Wave 1. Extends the same tiered_execution config resolution as tier-table-config (its sole dependency); no overlap with other items' files_scope.

narrative.Action: Add per-issue resolution to the tiered_execution config chain. An issue may set `tiered_execution: on|off` in its vBRIEF plan.metadata (mirroring how the swarm decides per-issue via the readiness analyzer, PAN-2203). Resolution order for whether an issue uses tiered-execution: per-issue override (if explicitly set) > global `tiered_execution.enabled` (default off). When the per-issue field is unset, inherit the global flag — zero change from today. Thread this resolved boolean into the tier-resolution chain so difficulty routing engages only for issues that (per-issue or globally) opted in.
narrative.Why: An all-or-nothing global switch is too coarse. Operators need to trial tiered-execution on specific issues without flipping it on globally, and to exclude specific issues when it is global. Mirrors the swarm's already-per-issue model.
narrative.AC: With global `tiered_execution.enabled=false` and an issue setting `tiered_execution=on`, that issue routes through the tier chain while others stay on the legacy path; with global `true` and an issue setting `tiered_execution=off`, that issue stays on the legacy path; unset inherits global. Covered by per-issue-tier-override.test.ts and asserted alongside the enablement-gate parity lock.

items.title: Add typed metadata.kind subject-matter field + planner emits it

narrative.Action: Add a typed `kind?: 'docs'|'api'|'backend'|'frontend'|'infra'|'test'|'refactor'|'design'|'spike'` to VBriefItemMetadata in src/lib/vbrief/types.ts (a loose `kind` bag already exists there — tighten it, do not duplicate). Update the planning prompt (src/lib/cloister/prompts/planning.md) to instruct the planner to emit `kind` per item, default 'backend' when unspecified. This is the optional byKind routing input for the resolution chain.

items.title: VBriefItemMetadata.kind is typed to the nine-value enum and typecheck passes for an item that sets kind:'docs'
An item with no kind still validates (field optional) and downstream code defaults to 'backend'
The planning prompt renders an instruction for the planner to emit kind per item and defaults to 'backend' when unspecified
Model+harness resolution chain: override -> byKind -> byDifficulty -> role default (fail-loud, no hardcoded fallback)

metadata.parallelSafeReason: Wave 1. Sole owner of the new resolve-tier.ts; reads only the wave-0 tier-table config and performs no shared writes.

parents.tier-table-config: Parent output defines src/lib/agents/tier-table.ts with TierDefinition, TieredExecutionConfig, ValidatedTieredExecutionConfig, DEFAULT_TIERED_EXECUTION_CONFIG, TieredExecutionConfigError, and validateTieredExecutionConfig(). Validation normalizes model ids, checks harness names, applies harness policy/ToS to tiers and supervisor, requires configured tiers to cover each difficulty exactly once, and exposes difficultyToTier as the resolver's difficulty lookup. Disabled/no-block config returns enabled=false, tiers={}, replay_threshold=0.5, difficultyToTier={}.
parents.kind-metadata-field: Parent output defines VBriefItemKind as docs|api|backend|frontend|infra|test|refactor|design|spike, DEFAULT_VBRIEF_ITEM_KIND='backend', and resolveVBriefItemKind(metadata) in src/lib/vbrief/types.ts. Planning prompt now requires metadata.kind on every plan item and says to default unclear or mixed categories to backend.

implementationNotes: resolveTier should be a pure function with no spawning or I/O.
Use the validated tier-table shape rather than revalidating model, harness, or difficulty coverage.
byKind entries should point to configured tier names and should win over difficultyToTier.
metadata.model override wins for model selection, but the implementation still needs an explicit harness source from byKind, difficultyToTier, or roleDefault.
If no path resolves and roleDefault is absent, throw a named error containing 'no tier/model configured' and do not include or return any hardcoded fallback model literal.

narrative.Action: Create src/lib/agents/resolve-tier.ts exporting resolveTier(item, config): { tierName, model, harness } implementing the resolution order: (1) per-bead metadata.model override, (2) config byKind[item.metadata.kind], (3) byDifficulty[item.metadata.difficulty] via the tier table, (4) role default. When nothing resolves and no default is configured, throw a clear 'no tier/model configured' error — NEVER return a hardcoded model literal (no-hardcoded-model-fallbacks rule). Pure function over the tier-table config from tier-table-config; no spawning here.

items.title: Given an item with metadata.model set, resolveTier returns that model regardless of kind/difficulty (the override wins)
Given no override but a byKind entry for the item's kind, resolveTier returns the byKind model and it wins over the byDifficulty model
Given only difficulty, resolveTier returns the tier whose difficulties[] contains it, with that tier's model and harness
Given an item that resolves to nothing and no configured default, resolveTier throws a named error and returns no model literal
Route the hardcoded complexityToModel / claude-only path through the new chain, preserving disabled-mode behavior

narrative.Action: Redirect the hardcoded difficulty->model switch complexityToModel() (src/lib/cloister/complexity.ts:314) and its caller router.ts:48 through resolveTier so there is one resolution path. Additive/no-loss: when tiered_execution is disabled, the resolved model for each difficulty must equal today's output (trivial/simple->haiku, medium/complex->sonnet, expert->opus). Do not delete complexityToModel until parity is proven by the lock test.

items.title: With tiering disabled, resolving each of the five difficulties returns exactly today's model (trivial/simple->haiku, medium/complex->sonnet, expert->opus)
router.ts resolves its model through resolveTier and no longer contains the inline complexityToModel switch
With tiering disabled, resolveTier returns the pre-change model for each difficulty and preserves the prior mapping
Wire resolved tier (model+harness) into slot dispatch so difficulty actually selects the worker

metadata.parallelSafeReason: Wave 2. Edits dispatch-tier.ts + spawn-prep.ts, disjoint from its wave-2 siblings (route-legacy touches complexity/router; supervisor-verdict touches tier-supervisor/specialists).

narrative.Action: Extend the chooseDispatchTier seam (src/lib/agents/dispatch-tier.ts) so that, when tiered_execution is enabled, it also yields the resolved (model, harness) from resolveTier — generalizing the binary 'in-context'|'registered-slot' into a tier assignment. Thread that model+harness into the slot spawn path (spawn-prep.ts model resolution) so a dispatched bead runs on the tier its difficulty selected. When disabled, behavior is unchanged. This is the fix for PAN-1196's 'difficulty captured and ignored'.

items.title: When tiered_execution is enabled, resolving a bead of difficulty=expert returns the expert tier's model+harness in the spawn params
When tiered_execution is disabled, chooseDispatchTier returns exactly its current 'in-context'|'registered-slot' result with no model override
A dispatched bead's spawn params carry the resolved model and harness (not the parent default) when tiering is on
Standing tier agents: spawn+track one long-lived registered slot per tier in the plan (lazy)

metadata.parallelSafeReason: Wave 3. Sole owner of the new standing-tiers.ts; its wave-3 sibling (fast-track-batching) owns a disjoint new file (fast-track.ts).

narrative.Action: Create src/lib/agents/standing-tiers.ts managing the set of standing tier agents for an issue. A standing tier agent is a long-lived registered slot spawned via spawnRun(issue,'work',{slotIndex,slotItemId}) that persists across many beads (not per-bead). Provide: which tiers the plan schedule needs (from the tier table + item difficulties — the tier-run scheduling half of FR-6), lazy spawn (a tier session spawns when its first bead is <=1 run away), a registry keyed by tier name, and a dispatchBeadToTier(tierName, bead) helper (unit-testable) that routes a bead to the standing slot. Reuse slot registration/branch conventions from spawn.ts; do NOT invent a new agent kind.

items.title: Given a plan schedule, the manager returns exactly the set of tier names it contains and spawns no tier absent from the schedule
A tier's standing session spawns lazily (when its first run is within one run) and is reused for subsequent beads of that tier rather than respawned
dispatchBeadToTier routes a bead to the registered slot for its tier and returns that slot's agent id
Each standing tier slot spawns as a recoverable registered agent (state file + tmux session) and the dashboard shows it under the issue
Commit feed: broadcast each commit diff to all standing tiers, ingestion-only

narrative.Action: Create src/lib/agents/tier-feed.ts. After each commit lands, broadcastCommit(sha, standingTiers) composes an ingestion-only feed message ('do NOT respond') containing the bead title and `git show <sha>` diff and delivers it to every standing tier agent via deliverAgentMessage (FR-7, no new transport). v1 is everyone-hears-everything: no per-tier filtering. Ingestion-only so per-event output cost is ~0. The feed for any agent must be reconstructible from git log + the standing-tier set (this is what replay consumes).

items.title: broadcastCommit sends the commit's diff to every standing tier agent via deliverAgentMessage (verified on a delivery spy)
Each feed message marks itself ingestion-only (instructs the recipient not to respond) and produces no response turn
Given N standing tiers and one commit, broadcastCommit sends exactly N deliveries (everyone hears everything, no filtering)
Foreman protocol: dispatch each bead to its tier's standing agent and broadcast the resulting commit

metadata.parallelSafeReason: Wave 5. Only wave-5 editor of roles/work.md and standing-tiers.ts; sequenced before supervisor-halt-dependents (the other roles/work.md editor) via a blocks edge.

narrative.Action: Extend the 'Foreman wave-driver protocol' in roles/work.md with a tiered path (gated by tiered_execution.enabled): when on, for each ready bead the foreman resolves its tier, dispatches to that tier's standing agent (standing-tiers.dispatchBeadToTier), stages+commits the result (one bead = one commit, foreman composes), then calls tier-feed.broadcastCommit so all standing tiers stay warm. Add any small orchestration helper to standing-tiers.ts (unit-testable) that this prose invokes, so verify has teeth. Encode the invariant as prose AND helper: only one implementation agent works a bead at a time (no worktree race).

items.title: roles/work.md contains the tiered dispatch loop (resolve tier -> dispatch to standing agent -> foreman commits -> broadcast feed), gated on tiered_execution.enabled
Each dispatched bead produces exactly one foreman-authored commit, preserving the one-bead-one-commit invariant
With tiered_execution disabled, roles/work.md preserves its existing serial and swarm paths and the default flow contains no edits
Standing supervisor agent: spawn + subscription policy (all | flagged | sampled)

narrative.Action: Create src/lib/agents/tier-supervisor.ts: spawn a standing supervisor agent (frontier model/harness from the tier table supervisor block) via spawnRun as a registered agent, and implement shouldSupervise(bead, policy): 'all' -> every commit; 'flagged' -> only requiresInspection beads; 'sampled' -> a configured rate. It never implements. Spawn/subscription only here; verdict posting is the next bead.

items.title: Under policy 'flagged', shouldSupervise returns true only for beads with requiresInspection=true
Under policy 'all', shouldSupervise returns true for every bead
The supervisor spawns as a registered, dashboard-discoverable agent using the tier-table supervisor model+harness
Supervisor reviews commit vs bead ACs and posts ack / blocking finding to the inspect-status surface

metadata.parallelSafeReason: Wave 2. Sequenced after supervisor-subscribe on tier-supervisor.ts; its wave-2 siblings (route-legacy, dispatch-model-selection) touch disjoint files.

narrative.Action: For each subscribed commit, deliver the diff + the bead's acceptance criteria (+ PRD FR text when metadata.traces present) to the supervisor, and land its ack or blocking finding on the EXISTING inspect-status surface (update.inspectStatus at specialists.ts:401; onInspectComplete at specialists.ts:514/527) so the foreman's existing wait-for-verdict path is reused unchanged. Preserve the existing rule that inspect failures do not change tracker status. Under tiered execution the push supervisor subsumes pull-based pan inspect.

items.title: A passing review persists an ack via the existing inspectStatus/onInspectComplete surface and saves the checkpoint (no new endpoint)
A failing review records a blocking finding on the inspect-status surface and does not change tracker (Linear/GitHub) status
For each subscribed commit, the supervisor receives the bead's acceptance criteria alongside the diff
A blocking supervisor finding halts dispatch of dependent beads until a fix commit lands

narrative.Action: Add a unit-testable shouldHaltDispatch(verdicts, nextBead, dag) to tier-supervisor.ts: returns true when an unresolved blocking finding exists on a bead that nextBead depends on (via blocks edges). Wire it into the foreman protocol in roles/work.md: when a blocking finding lands on a dependency of the next bead, the foreman dispatches the fix to the owning tier first (new commit), then continues — mirroring today's INSPECTION BLOCKED flow.

items.title: Given an unresolved blocking finding on bead X and a next bead that depends on X, shouldHaltDispatch returns true
Once a fix commit for X lands and is acked, shouldHaltDispatch returns false and dependent dispatch resumes
A blocking finding on a bead with no dependents permits unrelated dispatch (shouldHaltDispatch returns false)
Trivial fast-track: batch consecutive trivial mechanical beads into one dispatch (PAN-1311)

narrative.Action: Create src/lib/agents/fast-track.ts. groupFastTrack(beads): collect consecutive same-wave beads that are difficulty in {trivial,simple} AND scope-disjoint from siblings AND below a LOC threshold into a shared batch (fastTrackBatchKey). The 'skip slot dispatch' half already exists (chooseDispatchTier -> in-context); this adds batching so N trivial items become one dispatch instead of N. Honor files_scope to keep batches internally conflict-free.

items.title: groupFastTrack produces a batch of consecutive trivial, scope-disjoint beads that share one fastTrackBatchKey
groupFastTrack rejects a bead whose files_scope overlaps another in the candidate batch (no intra-batch conflicts)
groupFastTrack rejects any non-trivial (medium or harder) bead from a fast-track batch
Fail-open: a fast-track item that turns out non-trivial escalates to the standard tier

narrative.Action: In fast-track.ts, when a fast-track item is detected mid-flight to be non-trivial (verify fails in a way indicating real complexity, or the diff exceeds the LOC threshold), escalate it OUT of the fast-track batch to the standard tier with full review — fail-open, never fail-closed silently and never auto-merge it. Emit a signal the foreman acts on. Sequenced after fast-track-batching and before fast-track-automerge (the safety guard exists before auto-merge is enabled).

items.title: An item flagged non-trivial mid-flight exits its fast-track batch and routes to the standard tier
An escalated item routes through full review and the auto-merge path refuses it
Trivial-tier typecheck-gated auto-merge, skip convoy (config-gated, off by default)

metadata.parallelSafeReason: Wave 5. Sequenced after fast-track-escalation on fast-track.ts via a blocks edge; sole writer of slot-merge.ts among concurrent beads.

narrative.Action: Add a trivial-tier merge path that, ONLY when tiered_execution.enabled AND the batch is trivial+mechanical, runs a typecheck (and lint) gate and auto-merges the batch to the feature branch without the review convoy, reusing verifyAndMergeSlot machinery (slot-merge.ts) for the mechanics. TRUST BOUNDARY (hazard H1): this must be unreachable when disabled, must never apply to medium+ beads, and a failed gate must NOT merge. Default off.

items.title: With tiered_execution disabled, the auto-merge path refuses all items and every item routes through the normal review-then-merge flow
A trivial batch that passes typecheck and lint auto-merges to the feature branch without the review convoy
A trivial batch that fails the typecheck gate is not merged and surfaces back to the foreman
The auto-merge path rejects any batch that contains a medium-or-harder bead
Replay: respawn dead standing agent + re-deliver commit feed; threshold-triggered compaction at run boundaries

metadata.parallelSafeReason: Wave 5. Sole owner of the new tier-replay.ts; slot-reconcile.ts is not written by any concurrent bead.

narrative.Action: Create src/lib/agents/tier-replay.ts. replay(agent): respawn a dead standing tier agent or supervisor via spawnRun and re-deliver its feed reconstructed as `git log <base>..HEAD` (everyone-hears-everything => the full commit list) as batched ingestion-only messages, reaching dispatch-ready with no human intervention; build on slot-reconcile.ts. Triggers: (a) crash/orphan detection; (b) at a tier-run boundary, if estimated context exceeds replay_threshold (default 0.5) of the model window, kill+replay as compaction. NEVER compact mid-bead. Use fake timers for any delay in the tests (fake-timers rule). The supervisor replays via the same path (its feed = its subscription policy applied to git log).

items.title: When a standing tier agent is killed mid-issue, replay spawns a dispatch-ready session and re-sends the feed equal to git log base..HEAD in order
Replay triggers at a tier-run boundary when estimated context exceeds replay_threshold, and never triggers mid-bead
The supervisor replay spawns it through the same path and re-sends the subscription-filtered commit history
Measurement scaffolding: stamp feed deliveries + per-tier cost rows (warm-hit fraction)

narrative.Action: Create src/lib/agents/tier-metrics.ts. Stamp every feed delivery (from tier-feed.ts) with a timestamp + token count, and compute the warm-hit fraction (delivery-to-delivery gap < 300s) per standing agent. Ensure per-registered-agent cost attribution (foreman, each tier, supervisor) is available to `pan token-spend-report` (which already attributes cost per agent id). This is the instrumentation the later live pilot reads; the pilot RUN itself is out of scope (D10). Sequenced after commit-feed-delivery (shares tier-feed.ts).

items.title: Each feed delivery records a timestamp and token count queryable per standing agent
The system produces a per-agent warm-hit fraction (deliveries with gap < 300s) from the delivery log
pan token-spend-report shows distinct per-role rows for the foreman, each tier agent, and the supervisor
Enablement gate: with tiered_execution disabled, zero behavior change (parity lock test)

narrative.Action: Add a lock test asserting that with tiered_execution.enabled=false: resolveTier reproduces today's difficulty->model mapping; chooseDispatchTier returns its pre-change result; no standing tiers or supervisor are spawned; no auto-merge path is reachable. This is the mechanical guarantee that the whole feature ships dark. Restate the intent in the test name/comments.

items.title: With tiering disabled, resolveTier returns the legacy difficulty->model output for all five difficulties (locked by the test)
When disabled, the test verifies no standing-tier or supervisor spawns and no auto-merge branch executes
Document tiered execution (config, tiers, supervisor, fast-track, replay) in the docs site

narrative.Action: Write dev docs for tiered execution at docs/TIERED-EXECUTION.md: the tier table config shape, the resolution chain (override->kind->difficulty->default), standing warm tiers + everyone-hears-everything feed, the event-driven supervisor and its subscription policies, the trivial fast-track and its trust boundary, replay/compaction, and the off-by-default switch. Note the deferred non-goals (per-tier filtering, live pilot, remote tiers). If a Mintlify user-facing page is also wanted, mirror it under configuration/ (confirm the exact path in-repo).

items.title: The doc renders a fenced config example that contains the tiered_execution keys enabled, tiers, supervisor, and replay_threshold
The doc renders a Non-goals section that names per-tier commit filtering, the live cost pilot, and remote tiers as deferred
End-to-end: a fixture plan runs through tiered execution (warm tiers + supervisor + one fast-track batch)

metadata.parallelSafeReason: Wave 7 (terminal). Adds only a new e2e test file with no shared writes; runs after every producing bead has merged.

narrative.Action: Add an end-to-end test over a small fixture plan (mixing trivial, medium, expert beads) with tiered_execution enabled: assert difficulty routes each bead to the right tier's standing agent; every commit is broadcast to all standing tiers; the supervisor produces a verdict per subscribed commit and a blocking finding halts dependent dispatch; a trivial batch fast-tracks; and killing+replaying a standing agent recovers it. Use delivery/spawn spies (no real models); use fake timers for any delay per the fake-timers rule.

items.title: The e2e verifies each fixture bead routes to the standing agent of the tier its difficulty resolves
The e2e verifies every commit is broadcast to all standing tiers and the supervisor emits a verdict per subscribed commit
The e2e verifies a blocking supervisor finding blocks dependent dispatch until a fix commit, and a killed standing agent respawns to dispatch-ready
